Looks like a mirror less slr, fun! (edited)
Idk what that means lol
It came with a long and a short lens
Which do you recommend for everyday stuff
By the way the name of the camera is the Panasonic dc-g100dw-k if you are interested in its specs

SLR means Single Lense Reflex. This means that when you look through the viewfinder you actually see out the lense via mirrors. A mirrorless camera means they do away with the mirrors etc.  It's all digital.
What are the numbers on the Short and long lens?
Start with the smaller of the two lenses and see how it goes. Love ya!
Goodnight for reals!!

Interesting lens combo...  Ideally, I would like something like 18-80.  I have a 28-80 on my camera, which is pretty good for normal day to day use.  

Options:  

1.  Return it next P-Day.
2.  Get a different lens
3.  We can buy something and send it to you.

Love ya!

Kaden started a video call.

Victory or Death™
On February 24, 1836, with the garrison surrounded and the Texan Army at the Alamo outnumbered, one of the most famous letters in American history was written by William B. Travis. It was addressed, “To the People of Texas and All Americans in the World.” This letter was a passionate plea for aid for the Alamo garrison. He ended the letter “Victory or Death” – the only outcome this battle could have.

Commandancy of the The Alamo

Bejar, Feby. 24th. 1836

To the People of Texas & All Americans in the World-

Fellow Citizens & compatriots-

I am besieged, by a thousand or more of the Mexicans under Santa Anna - I have sustained a continual Bombardment & cannonade for 24 hours & have not lost a man - The enemy has demanded a surrender at discretion, otherwise, the garrison are to be put to the sword, if the fort is taken - I have answered the demand with a cannon shot, & our flag still waves proudly from the walls - I shall never surrender or retreat.  Then, I call on you in the name of Liberty, of patriotism & everything dear to the American character, to come to our aid, with all dispatch - The enemy is receiving reinforcements daily & will no doubt increase to three or four thousand in four or five days.  If this call is neglected, I am determined to sustain myself as long as possible & die like a soldier who never forgets what is due to his own honor & that of his country - Victory or Death.

William Barret Travis.

Lt. Col.comdt.

P. S.  The Lord is on our side - When the enemy appeared in sight we had not three bushels of corn - We have since found in deserted houses 80 or 90 bushels and got into the walls 20 or 30 head of Beeves.

Travis

Here's something that I've learned recently from my mission. Something that I think I'll use for the rest of my life. 

It goes perfectly with this quote from a conference talk that I love. I think it portrays very simply what it means to succeed. It can be so easy to get down on ourselves when we're not perfect. But ultimately, the only "perfect performance" that's ever been seen on this earth was Jesus Christ. I've realized recently that focusing solely on RISING AGAIN, no matter how long it takes to get back up, can help me view my spiritual progress in a more "celestial" manner. The Lord delights in plainness, and all he wants is our effort. And with His help, we can "get up one more time than we go down, and win."
This is from Mackay長老
I'll try to get something to you soon as well 👍
Thank you Mackay Chouro. Great perspective!  Did you tell him that effort is my favorite word?

Martinson長老 has been pretty transparent with his attitude and view of mission life

His primary objective is just not to go home, for fear of disappointment from those around him. He says that his family says there wouldn't be that, but he knows that he will be viewed with disappointment from his ward and even by his parents if they don't say so
He says he wants to get by while doing as little as possible
He will follow along with what I do which is great
But it's exhausting being the one to get us started every morning and every day
One more thing worth mentioning is that Martinson長老 and Mackay長老 are probably the least obedient missionaries in the mission 😂
They both have their own computers and will stay up playing video games
That also makes it harder to get started in the mornings
Martinson長老 is also very transparent about himself in the church. He acknowledges he probably won't stay a member for much after the mission either:(

1201 主の昇る日を讃えよ 1. 主が昇る日を讃えよ、ハレルヤ！天空の彼方の王座へ。ハレルヤ！罪人のために与えられた小羊、キリストが、ハレルヤ！今、いと高き天に入られた。ハレルヤ！ 2. そこに、主の高い勝利が待っている。ハレルヤ！永遠の門よ、頭を上げよ。ハレルヤ！主は死と罪を克服された。ハレルヤ！栄光の王を迎え入れよ。ハレルヤ！ 3. 見よ、主は両手を高く掲げておられる！ハレルヤ！見よ、主は愛の足跡を示される！ハレルヤ！聞け！主の恵み深い賜物を授けよ、ハレルヤ！下にある主の教会に祝福あれ。ハレルヤ！ 4. 主よ、われわれの死すべき目を超えたところへ、ハレルヤ！あなたの中に私たちの天国を見つけてください。ハレルヤ！
1201-Nushi no noboru hi o tataeyo 1.-Nushi ga noboru hi o tataeyo, hareruya! Tenkū no kanata no ōza e. Hareruya! Tsumibito no tame ni atae rareta shōyō, Kirisuto ga, hareruya! Ima, ito takaki ten ni haira reta. Hareruya! 2. Soko ni,-nushi no takai shōri ga matte iru. Hareruya! Eien no mon yo, atama o age yo. Hareruya! Omo wa shi to tsumi o kokufuku sa reta. Hareruya! Eikō no ō o mukaeire yo. Hareruya! 3. Miyo,-nushi wa ryōte o takaku kakagete ora reru! Hareruya! Miyo,-nushi wa ai no ashiato o shimesa reru! Hareruya! Kike! Omo no megumi fukai tamamono o sazukeyo, hareruya! Shita ni aru shunokyōkai ni shukufuku are. Hareruya! 4. Omo yo, wareware no shi subeki me o koeta tokoro e, hareruya! Anata no naka ni watashitachi no tengoku o mitsukete kudasai. Hareruya!

1201 Hail the Day That Sees Him Rise      1. Hail the day that sees Him rise, Alleluia! To His throne beyond the skies. Alleluia! Christ, the Lamb for sinners giv’n, Alleluia! Enters now the highest heav’n. Alleluia!   2. There for Him high triumph waits. Alleluia! Lift your heads, eternal gates. Alleluia! He has conquered death and sin; Alleluia! Take the King of Glory in. Alleluia!   3. See, He lifts His hands above! Alleluia! See, He shows the prints of love! Alleluia! Hark! His gracious gifts bestow, Alleluia! Blessings on His Church below. Alleluia!   4. Lord, beyond our mortal sight, Alleluia! Raise our hearts to reach Thy height. Alleluia! There Thy face unclouded see, Alleluia! Find our heav’n of heav’ns in Thee. Alleluia!

主が昇られるこの日をたたえよ、ハレルヤ！

あなたのうちに、天の天を見いださん、ハレルヤ！

This is Sister Whites email lol
You can just feel her energy through it 😂

皆さん、こんにちは！(Hello everyone!) This week was packed full of so much FUN!!! ✨️😍✨️ I never want to leave Fukuoka!!! My muscles are still aching from my week of adventures! I'm so out of shape. 😂 I need to work on that, or this summer is going to be BRUTAL! 🥵 Anyways, 今からレビューしましょう！(Let's review now!)

Last Monday for P-Day, me and Mahe姉妹 went to Canal City which is a HUGE MALL!!! 😍But because we have no money, it was more like window shopping. 🪟😂 Mahe姉妹 spent all of her money on a photo booth to take pictures with her favorite K-Pop artist. 💰 That girl would die for K-Pop. 😂 I also LOVE Japanese arcade games!!! We played this drum game 🥁 and it hurt to move my arms afterwards. I have so much respect for percussionists. Those anime songs we played with were SO FAST!!! 😰 We eventually wandered our way around the whole mall, and found a MINECRAFT POPUP STORE!!! 🤯 SAY LESS! I ZOOMED into that store like a baby zombie on a chicken (CHICKEN JOCKEY 🐔🧟‍♂️)!!! 😂 As a child, I yearned for the mines. 🫡⛏️ So OF COURSE I had to go in!!! And it was GLORIOUS!!! ✨️😍✨️ They had netherite swords, torches, axolotyl hats, anything you could ever imagine. I LOVE MINECRAFT!!! ⛏️💎 So that was indeed the highlight of my day. And the more I talk about this, the more I realize how nerdy I am. 🤓😂 Thank you to everyone for accepting me for who I am. 😂 Love Y'all! 🤠 

We biked through more rain this week. 🌧 I told Mahe姉妹 I wish I could be a passerby and watch myself bike, because I know my face looks like I'm in pain. 😂 But we found a cool shrine and took pictures next to it! I love living here. 🫶

We were SO BUSY on Wednesday!!! 🙌 We met up with a girl I found on Facebook! She is from Sri Lanka 🇱🇰 and is here as a college student! And she brought us chocolate mochi!!!! 🍫 SO KIND!!! We also had an appointment with an inactive family. I really want to teach the daughter and help her get baptized, but it's been hard to make appointments with them lately. But Wednesday was good!!! 😊 We were able to go and teach about the Atonement. And then we had to zoom our way over to the church for English class. 🇺🇸🦅 We had a lot of opportunities to meet with people that day, so I'm thankful. 😊

Every Thursday we have weekly planning, and I like to go to new cafes and try their hot chocolates. 😂🍫 So last Thursday, the one we went to also sold BAGELS!!! 🥯 THEY WERE SO GOOD!!! So now I need to go back and try all of the flavors!!! I got the cheese and salami bagel, and Mahe姉妹 got the chocolate one. SO YUMMY!!! I will definitely be back!!! 

Saturday was another busy day! We had activities all day! Last transfer, we could never go to activities because we never had any friends going. 😢 But we went to EVERYTHING last week! And guess who took more pictures with the Giant Robot??? 🤖 IT WAS ME!!! We had to go to Lala Port to play Futsal, which is just like soccer except on a smaller field. It was SO FUN!!! It honestly made me miss playing! ⚽️ And I wasn't half bad! I had a few good saves that I was proud of. 😊 But after that we had to bike 30 minutes back for dance class. 💃and I am not a dancer. 😂 And then we ended the day off at Mcdonalds. 🍔🍟 Japan Mcdonalds has this exclusive bacon and potato pie right now, and it's SO GOOD!!! 🥓🥔 Now they just have to bring over the Flint and Meal and then I'll be happy. 

We had a friend come to church on Sunday!!! Her name is Momiji and she plays the flute! 🪈 She is a professional! Graduated from college with a music degree and everything. 🤩 She is friends with one of our new members, Tsukasa兄弟. We OBVIOUSLY connected because we both love music! 🎶🤭 And she thinks I'm funny, so that's a win win. 🏆 I invited her to institute on Friday, and she wants to come!!! If she likes institute, I'm going to invite her to take gospel lessons. So everyone please pray that I can do that, because I'm lowkey freaking out. I hate rejection. Even though I get it every day. 😂 But it's different when the person is showing interest and then rejects you after. It stings a little more. But I can do this. She's awesome and because I care about her as a child of God that's all that matters. 😊

Side note, do I look like a water hose drinker??? 😂 Greep長老 says the water tastes weird, but me and Mahe姉妹 disagreed. So then he goes, "Yeah, you look like you drink from a water hose." IT TASTES GOOD! As a kid that was the best water I ever drank on hot summer days. 😂 I'd like to hear others thoughts. Please let me know lol.

The Japanese word of the week is koibana (恋ばな). It means Girl Talk, or Gossip! 🤭 

Scripture of the week: April 1980 General Conferece, N. Eldon Tanner, Celestial Marriages and Eternal Familes: "The happy home is one where the family lives together, works together, plays together, and prays together; where the parents show love and courtesy and demonstrate it to each other."

Thank you for reading! I hope this email was more exciting than last week's. Love you guys and have a great week!!! Always remember, God is good! 🫶

Sister Karson White
ホワイト姉妹

Love you kiddo!!

Tuesday
Today we were able to meet up again with Setsuko! We showed up at the park to talk and play Ground Golf and she showed up with a fresh haircut. She cuts her hair with scissors herself using two mirrors to see the back, shes awesome. She said we were both cute with our haircuts too. Before we played she recalled some of her memories she had of her two younger and very naughty brothers. When they were kids, their mom got a phone call from the school that the sons went too, and apparently one of them had broken all the windows Of the school 😈💀 one of them also sprayed a fire extinguisher Off of the top floor. That's a whole new level of rambunctious. The funniest story she told us (in her opinion) was when one of her brothers was 4 years old, he jumped straight into a pond trying to catch a butterfly with his net. Setsuko acted this out with a big jump which definitely surprised me considering she's 75 (I think). She also has arthritis so I don't really know how she's moving around so well, she's super energetic and I'm all here for it. We also got to exchange Line with her, so now we have a solid contact method!! We didn't get to talk much about gospel stuff but It was a good chance to get to know her and her background better. Afterwards we did Some housing and I used the flower method 🌸🌻🌹🏵🌷🥀🌼🌺💠💐 Basically we ting the doorbell at the house of someone with a lot of pretty flowers or something and I'll ask if it's OK for me to take a picture of them. Today we tried it and got to talk with some lady in her front yard for a solid 5-10 minutes! She showed us all her different flowers and their names, and we even talked a bit about our missions. We invited her to Eikaiwa and it seems like she had a little bit of interest, she's practiced English by listening to the radio before! Hopefully we get to meet her again!

We visited art store lady again! I need to get her name memorized, I heard it once but totally forgot. She was pretty busy helping a guy get some art stuff for a news broadcast (crazy) but she saw we had an interest in calligraphy and tried to help us get set up with some free classes with some random teacher. I also picked out some watercolor paper to try, and when she saw me trying to look for more coins to pay for it, she grabbed my hand and took only the money I had (it was less than the paper) and sent me out of the store with my paper 😭😊 shes so sweet but I literally want to like support the store. We will definitely visit again! We had Eikaiwa today, and before it started 2 random people (I think past students from like years ago) showed Up and wanted to say hi to us for a little while. We talked a bit about church stuff and a bit about Eikaiwa but it was super random. The two were maybe a couple (not sure) but they were for certain previously dance partners 💃🕺They were in their 70’s and weren't able to stay for long but it was nice to meet them. We played ping pong before Eikaiwa with one lady and she and I went back and forth for like 100 pings and tongs without missing a hit. It was like the spirit of ping pong was so strong 👻 Eikaiwa was fun, afterwards Shiko showed us his acoustic-electric guitar. It was super cool, and it blows my mind just how light they are compared to electric guitars lol. Today Setsuko also sent us a bunch of voice memos which are so cute and always in English. She told one of her friends about me and Magbago長老 and she wants to meet with us! From what Setsuko said she doesn't have much Of a gospel interest maybe, but Setsuko would like to talk about our beliefs all together with her. This will happen in 2 weeks, let's go!

Today we had exchanges with the Gobo elders, and I can say with surety that Gobo is the most Inaka (countryside) part of the mission. There's hardly anyone there. I was partnered up with Downs長老 and like 10 Percent of the doors we knocked even answered. One guy answered in a towel and had just gotten out of the bath. There was no reason he had to answer the door like that but it was awesome, props to him. Me and Matsuhisa長老 also played some Pokémon that night, and the game was like 2 hours long 😱 I ended up losing but I also chose not to just kill him in the first couple rounds so I feel validated. He also gave me much praise for my Japanese speaking, as a native he thinks I'm like cracked at Japanese. It's a very sweet sentiment, especially from another missionary who has likely seen all kinds of other missionaries and their own levels of Japanese. God has really blessed Me with an increased ability to speak and listen in Japanese as I have tried to align my will to His.

Today we had 3 Jimoty pickups, and because my comp don't got money ATM we biked to meet up with the 3 people. One of them was a bunch of assorted tools for 200¥, one of them was a couple guitar pickups for 1000¥, and another was a pair of 4kilogram exercise weights. Can you guess which one we picked up first?? The weights 💀 Can you guess How far we biked today? 30 Miles 💀💀💀 The whole time I carried around those weights and it was wild. Not sure I've biked a longer distance in one day In my whole life. Also having the weights made it significantly tougher. We crossed 4 cities in between meeting with all of those people. As usual we invited them all to our Eikaiwa classes and I left them positive reviews on Jimoty. It was crazy for real. I also got real crisped up From the sun, I will attach a picture. While biking home we tried to visit a house I’d been attempting to knock at least twice before. This time they answered the doorbell but immediately said no thanks even while I was saying how I loved their disney themed fence. Tragic. We also tried to say hi to a member by the church and she answered but we didn't exactly bring anything for her and she didn't have time for a longer visit so we gave a bit of an awkward greeting and left lol.

Sunday Surprise
At church we had a recently baptized member named Daniel show up to Sacrament meeting. He's been living in Japan for around 8 months and he's from Paraguay. We are hopefully gonna hang out with him on Pday! He lives pretty far and travels by bike so he hasn't gotten to attend church before now Since coming to Japan. What’s crazy is that he was tought and invited to baptism by a Sister Killian 😮 Here's something crazy. A day before we met Daniel, a sister missionary named Dossett姉妹 ♥️ sent me a Facebook profile for an Elder Kade Killian who was called to serve in Japan 3 ish years ago. We met Daniel and it was Elder Kade Killians sister who served in Paraguay and taught Daniel about the gospel. Insane divine inspiration from Dossett姉妹. Today me and Magbago played one of the card games he like called Union Arena And it was pretty fun. 

Random Missed note from last week, during exchanges met a high-school Girl on her porch and talked a bunch about Religion and what eternal life sounded like to her, and she even said that she imagines it would be living forever married 👀 

Pday was miserably rainy, weren't able to hang out with Daniel cause of it. we did bike a little ways to get groceries though. I bought a foot pedal thingy for my guitar and another CD. Not at the grocery store if that wasn't apparent Lol. We have plans to meet with Jiro tonight, I'll have to talk about how it goes next week 😁 (it went well, I'm writing this late and after we met with him. We Invited him to meet up with the missionaries in Australia And he seems like he's excited to!)

Spiritual thought
This week has been a week of huge progress as a companionship. Getting out and doing Missionary work feels so good. Things aren't perfect, and there's definitely times where we aren't using our Time 100 percent perfectly but it's way better than it has been. I can promise to each of you reading this that no matter what difficulties you are facing, God knows your trials and struggles. I have really tried to see things from his perspective recently, Maybe holding my tongue against my companion or waking him up if I don't want to 😂 (not happening all that often Fortunately) or following a prompting from the spirit. Things really seem much better as you are striving to Follow God's plan. It's different in each of our lives, so I'd invite you to pray and sincerely ask God what His plan looks like for you. Maybe even ask what you can do. I'm sure you'll be surprised by the response you get, I definitely have been.

I love each and every one of you! I'm grateful to be serving the Lord. I hope that my email can be a light to some one of my friends out there 😁

Elder Killian

But seriously, every time I read his journal, I’m amazed by how full it is of love—for your family, your friends, for God—and how grateful you are  for everything that happens.
 It always warms my heart and really inspires me.
 It reminds me that I need to be more grateful for all the things happening in my life too.
Lately, there’s someone I work with that I really struggle to get along with,
 and it’s been hard for me to work the same shifts as them.
 But reading Elder Kilian’s writing made me realize that maybe this is a trial God has given me,

even though I’m not a church member yet, I feel so supported knowing that God is always watching over me.
 Just thinking that God loves me gives me such strength.
 It’s like all those feelings of inferiority, or thinking that my parents love my siblings more than they love me—it all just stops mattering.
 And now, I don’t feel scared about things the way I used to—things like getting closer to others, being my authentic self, or stepping outside of my comfort zone.
 I’ve been able to do things that the old me would never have had the courage to do.
 So I really believe that there was a reason I met the missionaries.
 Thanks to all of them, I am who I am today, and I’m deeply grateful for that.

The Central Point
E (Chapters 36-42): The doctrinal heart of the book is Alma’s counsel to his sons. This section explains the “why” behind all the missions and wars. It contains the core doctrines of conversion, the plan of salvation, the spirit world, the resurrection, and the interplay of justice and mercy through the Atonement of Christ. The entire book pivots on this transfer of spiritual knowledge from a father to his children.
Let me do a deep analysis of just 36 for you.

Deep Dive Analysis of Alma Chapter 36
This document provides a detailed analysis of Alma chapter 36, focusing on its renowned chiastic structure. This chapter is one of the most powerful and intricate examples of chiasmus in all of scripture, serving as Alma’s personal testimony to his son Helaman about his conversion.

Overall Context
Alma 36 is a flashback, a personal story told from a father to a son. Alma recounts his dramatic conversion experience to impress upon Helaman the reality of sin, the necessity of repentance, and the profound joy of redemption through Jesus Christ. The structure of the chapter is not merely decorative; it is the key to its meaning. The literary form mirrors the spiritual content, with the entire narrative pivoting on the moment Alma turns his thoughts to the Savior.

The Chiastic Structure of Alma 36
This chapter is a near-perfect, extended chiasmus. The mirrored ideas and phrases are laid out below, moving from the outer layers to the central turning point.

A (v. 1-2): The Commandment to Remember God’s Deliverance

“My son, give ear to my words… I would that ye should do as I have done, in remembering the captivity of our fathers; for they were in bondage, and none could deliver them except it were the God of Abraham…”
B (v. 3): The Promise of Support and Prosperity in the Land

“…whosoever shall put their trust in God shall be supported in their trials, and their troubles, and their afflictions, and shall be lifted up at the last day.”
C (v. 4): Alma’s Testimony of God’s Power

“I know that God is not mocked; for he that knoweth these things of which I have spoken, and doth not them, the same is not saved.”
D (v. 5): The Story of Alma’s Opposition to the Church

“I had been born of God… I went about with the sons of Mosiah, seeking to destroy the church of God…”
E (v. 6-9): The Angelic Visitation and Confrontation

An angel of the Lord appears, speaking with a voice of thunder, causing the earth to shake. Alma is confronted for his rebellion.
F (v. 10): Alma’s Physical Collapse and Inability to Speak

“I was struck with such great fear and amazement that I fell to the earth and I did hear no more…”
G (v. 11): The Great Sin: Leading Others Astray

“I had rebelled against my God, and that I had not kept his holy commandments.”
H (v. 12-13): The Torment of a Damned Soul

“I was racked with eternal torment, for my soul was harrowed up to the greatest degree and racked with all my sins.”
I (v. 14): The Painful Memory of Sins

“Yea, I did remember all my sins and iniquities, for which I was tormented with the pains of hell…”
J (v. 15): The Desire for Annihilation

“…the very thought of coming into the presence of my God did rack my soul with inexpressible horror. Oh, thought I, that I could be banished and become extinct both soul and body…”
K (THE CENTER - v. 16-18): Remembering the Words of His Father and Turning to Christ

v. 16: “And it came to pass that as I was thus racked with torment, while I was harrowed up by the memory of my many sins…”
v. 17: “…behold, I remembered also to have heard my father prophesy unto the people concerning the coming of one Jesus Christ, a Son of God, to atone for the sins of the world.”
v. 18: “Now, as my mind caught hold upon this thought, I cried within my heart: O Jesus, thou Son of God, have mercy on me, who am in the gall of bitterness, and am encircled about by the everlasting chains of death.”
J’ (v. 19): The Absence of Pain

“And now, behold, when I thought this, I could remember my pains no more; yea, I was harrowed up by the memory of my sins no more.”
I’ (v. 20): The Marvelous Light and Joy

“And oh, what joy, and what marvelous light I did behold; yea, my soul was filled with joy as exceeding as was my pain!”
H’ (v. 21): The Sweetness of Being in God’s Presence

“Yea, and from that time even until now, I have labored without ceasing, that I might bring souls unto repentance; that I might bring them to taste of the exceeding joy of which I did taste; that they might also be born of God, and be filled with the Holy Ghost.”
G’ (v. 22): The Great Joy: Bringing Others to God

“Yea, and now behold, O my son, the Lord doth give me exceedingly great joy in the fruit of my labors; for because of the word which he has imparted unto me, behold, many have been born of God…”
F’ (v. 23-24): Alma’s Physical Recovery and Ability to Speak

“…I have been supported under trials and troubles of every kind… and I do put my trust in him, and he will still deliver me.”
E’ (v. 25): The Angelic Commission and Command

The angel commands Alma to go forth and not destroy the church, but to build it up.
D’ (v. 26): Alma’s Efforts to Build Up the Church

“…I have labored without ceasing, that I might bring souls unto repentance…”
C’ (v. 27): Alma’s Testimony of God’s Power to Deliver

“And I have been supported under trials and troubles of every kind, yea, and in all manner of afflictions; yea, God has delivered me from prison, and from bonds, and from death…”
B’ (v. 28): The Promise of Support and Being Lifted Up

“And I know that he will raise me up at the last day, to dwell with him in glory…”
A’ (v. 29-30): The Commandment to Keep the Records and Trust in God

“And now, my son, I command you that ye retain all my words, and that ye be diligent in keeping the commandments of God as they are written.”
Conclusion: Structure as Theology
Alma 36 is the ultimate example of form mirroring content. The entire narrative structure forces the reader to the central pivot point: the moment Alma’s mind catches hold of the thought of Jesus Christ. Everything before that moment is a descent into darkness, pain, and a desire for annihilation. Everything after it is an ascent into light, joy, and a desire to bring others to that same joy.

The chiasmus is not just a literary device; it is a theological statement. It teaches that a life without Christ spirals inward toward a black hole of guilt and despair. The only way to reverse that spiral is to turn to the Savior. His name is the turning point of the chapter and the turning point of a life. The perfectly mirrored structure demonstrates the completeness of this transformation—the joy is as profound as the pain was, the desire to build is as strong as the desire to destroy was, and the deliverance is as real as the bondage was.

If we dig even deeper it looks like there is a potential for a nested Chiastic Structure.  

Nested Chiastic Structure within Alma 36:6-10
Within the larger chiasmus, the section describing the angelic visitation and Alma’s immediate reaction (verses 6-10) also contains a subtle, yet discernible, nested chiastic structure. This smaller chiasmus emphasizes the angel’s powerful confrontation and Alma’s immediate cessation of his destructive path.

A (v. 6): Angel sent to stop Alma from destroying the church.
B (v. 7): Angel speaks with a voice of thunder, causing the earth to shake.
C (v. 8): So great was the voice, it shook the whole earth, and they fell to the earth from fear.
D (v. 9): The voice directly confronts Alma: “why persecutest thou the church of God?” and declares the Lord’s church will be established.
C’ (v. 10): The voice continues, stating Alma’s actions have darkened the lights of the world because he sought to destroy the church and rejected prophets.
B’ (v. 10): The voice commands Alma to “seek no more to destroy the church of God, but rather seek to build it up.”
A’ (v. 10): Alma is struck with fear and amazement, falls to the earth, and hears no more, effectively stopping his destructive path.
